title: content.rs hashtag inside-a-tag guard has broken boolean logic
description: src/content.rs line 71: if !before.ends_with("href=\"") && !before.contains("") { Due to operator precedence, this parses as: (!ends_with_href && !contains_open_a) || contains_close_a This means: if there is ANY anywhere before the match position, the hashtag will always be linked — even if it is inside a currently-open tag later in the string. The intent was likely: if !before.ends_with("href=\"") && (!before.contains("")) meaning 'not inside an a tag = either no opened, or the last was closed.' What goes wrong: hashtags that appear inside an existing tag after the first in the document will be double-linked, producing malformed HTML like . This is a correctness bug, not just readability, but the root cause is that the boolean expression requires a re-read and the correct version needs a parenthesized comment explaining the intent.
